channel.title: Frontity Embedded Mode's Wordpress Toolbar
channel.description:
<!--
Are you asking about "how to solve a specific issue/error/problem in your Frontity project"?
If so, please check this guide on the information we suggest you include in your topic so the community (and the Frontity team) can provide the best possible help as quickly and as efficiently as possible
https://community.frontity.org/t/about-the-dev-talk-questions-category/2/3
The info you may provide: - Description of your issue - Specific errors you’re getting in the terminal or in the browser’s console - A repository with the code of your project - A deployed version of your site - The URL of your WP REST API
-->
Hey everyone! I'm VERY new to the whole Frontity space, but finding this framework was such a blessing fr. It means I don't need to spend my summer learning PHP and instead can work in React.
I've just started working in embedded mode and I'm seeing an issue with the WP toolbar. Instead of displaying at the top with styles, it's currently breaking as can be seen in the screenshot below: ![nique.local_|666x500](upload://4ArdbtZMCJ7dbPdz4XU9laxE3Uo.png)
``` $ npx frontity info
## System: - OS: macOS 12.0.1 - CPU: (8) x64 Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8569U CPU @ 2.80GHz - Memory: 697.57 MB / 16.00 GB - Shell: 5.8 - /bin/zsh ## Binaries: - Node: 12.22.10 - ~/.nvm/versions/node/v12.22.10/bin/node - npm: 6.14.16 - ~/.nvm/versions/node/v12.22.10/bin/npm ## Browsers: - Chrome: 102.0.5005.61 - Edge: Not Found - Firefox: 84.0.2 - Safari: 15.1 ## npmPackages: - @frontity/core: ^1.15.1 => 1.15.1 - @frontity/html2react: ^1.7.0 => 1.7.0 - @frontity/mars-theme: ./packages/mars-theme => 1.6.2 - @frontity/tiny-router: ^1.4.4 => 1.4.4 - @frontity/wp-source: ^1.11.7 => 1.11.7 - frontity: ^1.17.2 => 1.17.2 ## npmGlobalPackages: - frontity: 1.17.2 - npx: Not Found ``` I'm using Local to set up a local wordpress site for dev purposes— as such, I don't have a lot of the other URLs etc publicly accessible but I can answer any questions you might have! I'm also on WP6.0 if that makes a difference.

item.title: Frontity Embedded Mode's Wordpress Toolbar
item.dc:creator: dishpanda
item.description:
Hey, just following up— do you have any idea why this might be happening?

Read full topic

item.title: Frontity Embedded Mode's Wordpress Toolbar
item.dc:creator: dishpanda
item.description:
Yeah for sure! I don’t see any 404s though unfortunately.

Read full topic

item.title: Frontity Embedded Mode's Wordpress Toolbar
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
Hi @dishpanda, could you send a screenshot of the network tab when you load that page? Just to see if the styles request is returning 404. Maybe something changed with WP 6.0.

Read full topic

item.title: Frontity Embedded Mode's Wordpress Toolbar
item.dc:creator: dishpanda
item.description:
Hey everyone! I’m VERY new to the whole Frontity space, but finding this framework was such a blessing fr. It means I don’t need to spend my summer learning PHP and instead can work in React.

I’ve just started working in embedded mode and I’m seeing an issue with the WP toolbar. Instead of displaying at the top with styles, it’s currently breaking as can be seen in the screenshot below:

$ npx frontity info

## System:
 - OS: macOS 12.0.1
 - CPU: (8) x64 Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8569U CPU @ 2.80GHz
 - Memory: 697.57 MB / 16.00 GB
 - Shell: 5.8 - /bin/zsh
## Binaries:
 - Node: 12.22.10 - ~/.nvm/versions/node/v12.22.10/bin/node
 - npm: 6.14.16 - ~/.nvm/versions/node/v12.22.10/bin/npm
## Browsers:
 - Chrome: 102.0.5005.61
 - Edge: Not Found
 - Firefox: 84.0.2
 - Safari: 15.1
## npmPackages:
 - @frontity/core: ^1.15.1 => 1.15.1 
 - @frontity/html2react: ^1.7.0 => 1.7.0 
 - @frontity/mars-theme: ./packages/mars-theme => 1.6.2 
 - @frontity/tiny-router: ^1.4.4 => 1.4.4 
 - @frontity/wp-source: ^1.11.7 => 1.11.7 
 - frontity: ^1.17.2 => 1.17.2 
## npmGlobalPackages:
 - frontity: 1.17.2
 - npx: Not Found

I’m using Local to set up a local wordpress site for dev purposes— as such, I don’t have a lot of the other URLs etc publicly accessible but I can answer any questions you might have! I’m also on WP6.0 if that makes a difference.

Read full topic
